title: RNA extraction from Aiptasia larvae with the Zymo Research Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bcbsu","text":"This protocol is for extraction of high-quality total RNA from Aiptasia larvae to use in NGS library preparations and RT-qPCRs. The protocol is written for the Zymo Research Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit (D7005) but can also be adapted for the Zymo Research Quick-RNA Microprep Kit, the additional reagents needed are listed. This protocol works well with ≥200 larvae between 1 dpf and 9 dpf and should produce ~100-500 ng total RNA, for older/smaller larvae, higher numbers might be required. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7bddk","text":"The use of Proteinase K is not strictly required but results in a\nhigher RNA yield.  The initial Zymo-Spin IC-XM column step originates from the simultaneous extraction of RNA AND DNA and takes some load from the later DNase I digestion by removing most DNA from\nthe sample but is not required.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":176,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhc38","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qvqi","text":"This protocol works well with ≥200 larvae and should produce ~100-500 ng total RNA.  If the larvae are not fed and therefore shrinking over time, higher numbers are required after approximately 9 dpf; good starting points are ≥500 larvae for 10-15 dpf, ≥1000 for even older larvae, but it might be worth determining the optimal numbers experimentally before spending time and resources in a big experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uae","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e7jgs","text":"All volumes in the protocol are for 40 µL of ASW containing the required number of larvae plus 120 µL of 1× DNA/RNA Shield.  This results in a total volume of 160 µL and a 3:1 DNA/RNA Shield to sample ratio.  If using larger numbers of larvae in larger volumes, recalculate accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":286}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"adv73","text":"Preparation of reagents before first usage:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"432rp","text":"Add 96 mL 100% ethanol (104 mL 95% ethanol) to the 24 mL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer concentrate (D7005).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdb3g","text":"Reconstitute lyophilized DNase I [E1009-A (250 U)] with 275 µL nuclease-free water and mix by gentle inversion.  Use immediately or store aliquots at −20°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ts0b","text":"Reconstitute lyophilized Proteinase K [D3001-2-20 (60 mg)] at 20 mg/mL with 3.12 mL Proteinase K Storage Buffer and mix by vortexing.  Use immediately or store aliquots at −20°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e9vha","text":"Prepare a 1× solution of DNA/RNA Shield: add 25 mL of nuclease-free water to the\n25 mL of the 2× DNA/RNA Shield concentrate (R1200-25) and mix well.  Store at RT.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a83tp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9kekl","text":"Preparations before taking samples:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k5gq","text":"Label clean 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes for all samples to be taken (these need to be free of contaminants but not necessarily sterile or certified RNase/DNase free).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7j947","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ked7","text":"Preparation before each round of extractions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fucdk","text":"Tubes and spin columns:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6emgs","text":"­Nuclease-free 1.5-mL tubes (3 sets for all samples plus one for the DNase I reaction mix)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8vk1","text":"One more set if doing the optional Zymo-Spin IC-XM column step to remove DNA at the beginning of the procedure.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ebjeu","text":"Label all tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d14dk","text":"­Zymo-Spin IC columns (clear) in 2-mL collection tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"brio1","text":"Label the column lids.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"37s7s","text":"OPTIONAL: Zymo-Spin IC-XM columns (yellow)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cr6qd","text":"Place the Zymo-Spin IC-XM tubes in one set of the labelled nuclease-free tubes and label column lids as well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1o2n4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e300d","text":"Prepare a DNase I reaction mix in the labelled tube:  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aos00","text":"Add 5 µL DNase I and 35 µL DNA Digestion Buffer per sample and calculate with one extra sample as buffer. Keep on ice until used.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4md5j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5rqn","text":"Reagents to have ready for extractions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":39}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4ge1","text":"­100% ethanol (Molecular Biology Grade), ~400 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6mmsl","text":"Nuclease-free water (kit, better use 2-mL aliquots from IDT: 11-04-02-01), ~15 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"57eq5","text":"Heat an aliquot of the nuclease-free water to 65°C for the final elution step.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jgmi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"777m4","text":"­DNA/RNA Lysis buffer (kit, RT), ~200 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5hstm","text":"DNA/RNA Wash Buffer (kit, RT), ~1500 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"du7qp","text":"DNase I (kit, stored at −20°C, thaw, keep on ice), ~5 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mhuf","text":"DNA Digestion Buffer (kit, RT) , ~35 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v8dl","text":"­DNA/RNA Prep Buffer (kit, RT), ~400/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kecd","text":"­Proteinase K (kit, stored at −20°C, thaw, keep on ice), ~8 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7b5bi","text":"PK Digestion Buffer (kit, RT), ~16 µL/sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7uath","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4630u","text":"After the initial sampling, use only RNase-free reagents and consumables and follow general rules for the work with RNA such as use of regularly changed gloves, treatment of any surfaces touched during the extraction with RNase decontamination solution (e.g., RNase Away or RNaseZAP) and keeping the work area clean and tidy. NEB has a nice guide here: https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":353,"length":98}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2C08B643423211F0AB870A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination"}}}}

(5 mL for 50 preps – R1200-1-5 or 20 mL for 200 preps - R1200-1-20)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3okap","text":"Molecular Biology Grade 100% ethanol (~130 mL for 50 preps or ~550 mL for 200)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mq1c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fm25","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aelea","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"am0q8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C3D1D7B84B3911F0BED10A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.zymoresearch.com/collections/quick-dna-rna-kits/products/quick-dna-rna-microprep-plus-kit"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B9996C804B3911F0936B0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.zymoresearch.com/collections/quick-rna-kits/products/quick-rna-microprep-kit"}}}}
steps: Transfer ~200 larvae in 40 µL ASW into a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Use a 10-µL or a 20-µL pipet which uses 10-µL XL tips, e.g., Eppendorf Research Plus 2 – 20 µL, light gray, 3123000098. 20 µl should allow to take up 20-30 larvae with practice, to concentrate them further do a series of pipetting steps into smaller and smaller drops of ASW on a new polystyrene surface (e.g., lid of a 60-mm disposable Petri Dish).
Thaw the samples at RT and wait until they have reached RT themselves. Vortex thoroughly.

Finish taking all samples, then store them at −80°C until extractions. This assumes that further samples need to be taken at other days. I only taking samples for a single time point, keep them at RT and proceed with the RNA extraction.
Add 120 µL 1× DNA/RNA Shield and vortex briefly.
OPTIONAL: Add 8 µL Proteinase K and 16 µL PK Digestion Buffer to the 160-µL samples. In our experience, this increases the RNA yield. If processing multiple samples make a master mix.
Add 368 µL 100% ethanol to the now 368-µL sample and mix well.
Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. SAVE FLOW-THROUGH! (That’s where the RNA is!)

OPTIONAL: Transfer the sample into a Zymo-Spin IC-XM Column (yellow) sitting in a 1.5-mL nuclease-free tube. This is only if you are using the Quick-DNA/RNA Microprep Plus Kit and is not required but makes life easier on the DNase I step later on.
Add 184 DNA/RNA Lysis Buffer to the samples (equal to sample volume), mix well.
OPTIONAL: Mix well and incubate at 25°C for 30 min.
Transfer sample to Zymo-Spin IC Column (clear) sitting in a 2-mL collection tube.
Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. Discard flow-through.
Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT. Discard flow-through.
Add 40 µL of the DNase I reaction mix (see "Before start" instructions) directly to the column matrix and incubate for 30 min at 25°C.
Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Prep Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.
Add 700 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.
Add 400 µL DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column, spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT and discard flow-through.
Carefully transfer the dry column into a new nuclease-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 15 µL 65°C-warm nuclease-free water directly to the column and spin at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT.
Add the eluate onto the column again and spin at at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT.
Transfer the eluate into a fresh nuclease-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube. Take aliquot for quality control if desired and store remaining RNA at −80°C or proceed with downstream application.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
